category: Occupational fraud - procurement corruption and bid-rigging
risk_signals: Repeated awards to a single vendor, or bids clustered just below competitive-tender thresholds
Purchases split to stay under approval or tender limits
Undisclosed relationships between procurement staff and winning vendors
analyst_reasoning: Procurement fraud manipulates the buying process through kickbacks, bid-rigging, or conflicts of interest. As a corruption scheme it hides in seemingly valid contracts; the diagnostic patterns are competition that is not genuinely competitive and purchases structured to evade oversight thresholds.
investigation_steps: Analyse award concentration and win rates by vendor for signs of rigging
Detect split purchases and bids clustered just under tender/approval thresholds
Screen for conflicts of interest between procurement staff and vendors
decision: Re-tender affected contracts, enforce conflict-of-interest declarations, and pursue action on confirmed collusion or kickbacks.
outcome: Procurement corruption inflates costs quietly over time; competition analytics and conflict checks are the main controls.
lessons_learned: Single-vendor award concentration and near-threshold bids indicate rigging
Split purchases are a deliberate device to evade oversight
Conflict-of-interest disclosure is a core preventive control in procurement
